response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and deep large-scale distributed/cloud compute platform leadership. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014-2015) and no evidence of building large-scale cloud compute infrastructure or formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers. Primary stacks are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which do not satisfy the core requirement.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with founder-level ownership (GISCollective) and exposure to high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship orientation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops, and mentoring through talks and courses
Polyglot breadth across backend, web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps tooling (Docker, GitLab CI, Kubernetes deployments)
concerns: Does not meet the 8-year C++ minimum — only a short C/C++ role at National Instruments and a C++ bachelor-thesis project
No demonstrated experience leading or architecting an internal cloud compute platform at scale; cloud work is AWS CDK product-level, not compute-platform-level
No explicit technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers; leadership shown is mostly community/teaching, not org-level tech lead

key_factors.factor: Lack of large-scale cloud compute platform / distributed infra leadership
Strong craftsmanship, mentoring, and high-throughput systems exposure
